results.name: Iris Energy
keyThemes: Gigawatt-scale power capacity as scarce asset
Undervaluation relative to infrastructure build cost
results.topBullish:
Leaked Anthropic docs reveal plans to buy 1.4GW of Aussie DC capacity -AFR
$IREN just announced on June 3rd its first-ever project in Australia: 800 MW AI data center campus in Bundey, South Australia.  Is Anthropic and IREN deal finally coming?
results.topBearish:
The $IREN grant isn't just big.  Look at who recommended it.
The comp committee that blessed ~5% of the company to the co-CEOs (no hurdles, no vote) includes the stock's most vocal online promoter, who the proxy shows is separately paid $50K to represent IREN at investor https://t.co/xspa4cExmY
results.summary: Sentiment around $IREN is predominantly bullish, driven by leaked Anthropic documents indicating a search for 1.4GW of Australian data center capacity that closely aligns with IREN's newly announced 800MW Bundey, South Australia campus, positioning the company as a prime hyperscaler deal candidate. Key bear risks include timeline skepticism around the 2028 energization window potentially disqualifying IREN from near-term Anthropic contracts, alongside governance red flags involving co-CEO equity grants with no performance hurdles and a compensated board promoter.

results.name: Tesla
keyThemes: Q2 record deliveries vs. stock selloff paradox
results.topBullish: $TSLA  just posted its best quarter ever. The stock had its worst day in a year anyway. 480,126 vehicles delivered in Q2 — up 25% year-over-year, beating Wall Street's estimate of 406,000 by a full 18%. By any normal read, that's a blowout quarter. The stock fell 7.5% the same
results.topBearish: "Michael Burry just disclosed short positions against $TSLA, Nvidia, and Micron — right after Tesla posted record Q2 deliveries of 480,126. The stock still dropped 7.5%. A classic Wall Street paradox. Is Burry early or is the market right? #Tesla"
results.summary: Tesla's social feed is heavily polluted with spam and promotional content, obscuring genuine signal, but the substantive posts reveal a notable disconnect: record Q2 deliveries of 480,126 units (+25% YoY, +18% vs. estimates) failed to prevent a 7.5% single-day stock decline, raising concerns about valuation and sell-the-news dynamics. Headwinds include Michael Burry's disclosed short position and EU regulatory barriers blocking supervised FSD, while a small cohort of long-term bulls maintain conviction in Tesla's 10-year trajectory.
